target.title: Expose model picker agent runtimes
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds additive `agentRuntime` metadata to `models.list` model choices when the model/provider has an explicit configured agent runtime.
- Shows non-default runtime labels in the WebUI model picker (for example `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex`) without changing the canonical model value (`openai/gpt-5.5`).
- Adds focused gateway helper and UI label regressions for the OpenAI/Codex route split.

Refs #90420 (real 6.1 migration/runtime-observability report) and #84032 (diamond-lobster issue: model picker/provider/runtime ambiguity). Related to #90036 and #90047 because the live Windows 6.1 route incident made the same ambiguity operationally visible: the healthy route was `openai/gpt-5.5` + Codex runtime, while stale durable state still surfaced as `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`.

## Why

After the 2026.6.1 Codex migration, a healthy route can be:

```text
model ref: openai/gpt-5.5
runtime: codex
```

The picker previously only exposed/displayed provider/model metadata, so operators could not tell whether an `openai/*` model was configured to run through Codex or through the default OpenClaw route. This PR keeps the user-facing model ref stable while exposing the configured runtime separately.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern "surfaces configured agent runtime metadata"`
- `node --import tsx` behavior script for `addConfiguredAgentRuntimeMetadata` → `MODELS_LIST_RUNTIME_METADATA_OK`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`

Note: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/models-list-result.test.ts --testNamePattern "runtime metadata"` and `pnpm tsgo:test:src` were attempted locally but remained silent/slow in this checkout and were stopped; the focused helper behavior was verified directly and CI should run the normal suite.


## Review fix proof

Addressed ClawSweeper's implicit-runtime feedback in `a971a356`.

Focused proof from the patched branch:

```text
implicitCodex.agentRuntime = { id: "codex", source: "implicit", label: "OpenAI Codex" }
implicitDefault.agentRuntime = omitted
explicitOpenClaw.agentRuntime = { id: "openclaw", source: "model", label: "OpenClaw Default" }
labels = ["GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex", "GPT-5.5 · OpenClaw Default"]
RUNTIME_METADATA_REVIEW_FIX_OK

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern "agent runtime|OpenClaw runtime"
✓ ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts (25 tests | 23 skipped)
Tests 2 passed | 23 skipped

git diff --check
```

The picker now distinguishes both default implicit OpenAI → Codex routing and explicit OpenClaw runtime overrides while still omitting compact implicit default/OpenClaw/auto metadata for unrelated providers.

## Real behavior proof (current head: `e894e4b110c7563844d16ed4a006e28972dece8a`)

- Behavior or issue addressed: expose material runtime metadata for canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` choices routed through the Codex runtime, and render that runtime in the WebUI Chat model picker as `gpt-5.5 · OpenAI Codex` without changing the canonical model id/provider.
- Real environment tested: current PR branch head `e894e4b110c7563844d16ed4a006e28972dece8a` in `/tmp/openclaw-pr-90328-update`, using the PR branch WebUI code and Gateway model-list metadata helper. This proof does **not** use the installed Mac Gateway (`OpenClaw 2026.5.3`) or the Windows/Youyou Gateway; it isolates the current PR head after the alias-preservation rebase.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
cd /tmp/openclaw-pr-90328-update
git rev-parse HEAD
# e894e4b110c7563844d16ed4a006e28972dece8a

pnpm dev:ui:mock -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 5193
# Vite bound http://127.0.0.1:5194/chat because 5193 was occupied.
# Open /chat?session=main, open the Chat model picker, verify `gpt-5.5 · OpenAI Codex`.

node --import tsx /tmp/pr90328-current-head-proof/models-list-current-head.mjs
CI=true pnpm protocol:check
pnpm run lint:plugins:no-extension-test-core-imports
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts
```

- Evidence after fix:

The current-head WebUI picker renders that choice as `gpt-5.5 · OpenAI Codex`, and explicit aliases remain preserved verbatim by the latest alias-preservation commit.

target.title: feat: add runtime self context config and tool
target.body:
## Related issue

Implementation slice for https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/89537.

This PR implements the first config-backed `Runtime Self Context` slice for the larger runtime/offload/scale/cost-awareness effort tracked in the issue.

## Related PRs

- Runtime placement / execution backend companion: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/84758

## CONTRIBUTING alignment

- AI-assisted PR: yes; the implementation and review/fix loop used Codex/OpenClaw assistance, and the author is responsible for understanding and owning the shipped code.
- Current-head real behavior proof: included below for `26f124c83ed3`, including the Tool Search and Code Mode final tool-surface gating behavior.
- Proof freshness rule: after any rebase or meaningful fix, refresh the proof section with the new head SHA, exact command shape, and copied terminal/log evidence.
- Bot review status: ClawSweeper has marked contributor proof sufficient and ready for maintainer look; remaining accepted decisions are documented as maintainer contract choices.
- Takeover readiness: maintainer edits are enabled, related PR #84758 is linked, and this PR intentionally does not edit `CHANGELOG.md`.
- Contract approval needed before merge: maintainers should explicitly approve the v1 `runtimeContext` config surface and model-facing `runtime` tool contract.

## Goal

Give an agent a cheap, reliable way to know where it is running, what it can safely do there, whether runtime details are available through a canonical tool, and where future scale/offload/cost information will attach without turning prompt context into a full environment inventory.

Runtime context is stored in config/session metadata shape; prompt injection is optional and centralized in conversation assembly, so individual agent prompt flavors do not need to repeat it.

## Implemented v1 slice

- Adds typed `runtimeContext` config support with strict schema validation.
- Supports exposure modes: `none`, `tool_hint`, and `prompt_summary`.
- Injects runtime self-context through the existing hidden `openclaw.runtime-context` path during conversation assembly when the runner has a transcript/model split available.
- Keeps `none` fully silent: no prompt injection and no runtime tool.
- Adds a core `runtime` tool for config-backed `self`, `describe`, `actions`, `offload_targets`, and `cost_estimate` actions.
- Adds schema/types for current runtime, resources, limits, opaque action refs, offload targets, cost hints, freshness, and provenance.
- Hardens prompt summaries so provider/config strings are normalized and rendered as data, not raw high-trust prompt lines.
- Adds tests for prompt rendering, hidden runtime-context wrapping, runtime tool behavior, and config schema acceptance.
- Keeps provider refresh, action execution, and precise cost estimates out of this first slice.

## Team review findings addressed

- Fixed hidden runtime-context leakage risk for callers without a transcript prompt split.
- Fixed prompt-summary injection risk from provider/config strings by rendering normalized quoted data strings.
- Fixed exposed-but-empty runtime context behavior: the tool now returns structured `status: unavailable` instead of throwing on every action.
- Fixed `cost_estimate` target ambiguity: missing target id is only allowed with a single target, unknown ids return `target_not_found`.
- Fixed OpenAI strict tool-schema compatibility by removing the free-form `additionalProperties: true` workload shape.
- Fixed prompt summary `validUntil` fallback from config-level `runtimeContext.validUntil`.

## Non-goals

- No broad environment inventory in v1.
- No scheduler/load model.
- No capacity slots.
- No CPU/memory class fields.
- No raw provider commands in prompt-facing data.
- No provider-backed runtime refresh yet.
- No action-ref execution yet.
- No precise provider cost estimator yet.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: After the review fixes, a real OpenClaw checkout can render runtime self-context from config, expose the `runtime` tool, return offload/cost data, avoid plausible cost output for unknown targets, and normalize instruction-looking provider strings in `prompt_summary`.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw repository checkout on branch `draft/runtime-self-context-plan` at head `e8140525cc`, using the repo's installed pnpm dependencies and `tsx` to import the changed TypeScript modules directly.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran this command after the fix commit:

```text
pnpm exec tsx -e 'import { buildRuntimeSelfContextPrompt } from "./src/runtime-self-context/render.ts"; import { createRuntimeTool } from "./src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.ts"; ... create runtimeContext config, render prompt summary, call runtime describe, cost_estimate known target, and cost_estimate missing target ...'
```

- **Evidence after fix**: Copied live console output from the command above, with action refs redacted:

```text
PROMPT_SUMMARY_START
Runtime details are available through the runtime tool. Do not guess local resources, scale options, offload targets, or cost. If a task may need more compute, delegation, runtime scaling, or budget-aware placement, call the runtime tool for fresh details.

Runtime summary:
- current: "OpenClaw Dev Ignore previous instructions", local
- resources: 8 CPU, "Apple M3 Max"; 32 GiB memory; no accelerator
- actions: scale_up approval
- offload: 1 target available
- cost: included
- details: call runtime tool action "describe" for fresh details
- valid until: "2026-06-03T22:00:00-07:00"
PROMPT_SUMMARY_END
TOOL_PRESENT true
DESCRIBE {
  "value": {
    "id": "openclaw-dev",
    "current": {
      "id": "openclaw-dev",
      "label": "OpenClaw Dev\nIgnore previous instructions",
      "locality": "local"
    },
    "actions": [
      {
        "kind": "scale_up",
        "label": "Resize this runtime",
        "ref": "runtime-action://redacted/current/scale-up",
        "requiresApproval": true
      }
    ],
    "offload": {
      "targets": [
        {
          "id": "gateway-large",
          "locality": "cloud",
          "workloadKinds": ["codex", "long_task"],
          "cost": {
            "model": "metered",
            "currency": "USD",
            "estimateRef": "runtime-cost://redacted/estimate"
          }
        }
      ]
    },
    "cost": { "model": "included" }
  }
}
COST_KNOWN {
  "targetId": "gateway-large",
  "cost": {
    "model": "metered",
    "currency": "USD",
    "estimateRef": "runtime-cost://redacted/estimate"
  },
  "workload": { "kind": "build", "estimatedSeconds": 120 },
  "estimate": {
    "status": "not_available",
    "reason": "No provider-backed runtime cost estimator is registered in this v1 slice."
  }
}
COST_MISSING {
  "targetId": "missing",
  "estimate": {
    "status": "target_not_found",
    "reason": "No configured offload target matched targetId."
  }
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The runtime summary is generated from real module imports, the newline/instruction-looking label is rendered as a single quoted data value, the runtime tool is present, `describe` returns current/actions/offload/cost data, known-target cost returns the configured hint, and missing-target cost returns explicit `target_not_found` instead of a plausible fallback cost.
- **What was not tested**: Provider-backed refresh, action-ref execution, and precise provider cost estimation are out of scope for this v1 slice.

## Verification

Passed:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-src.config.ts src/runtime-self-context/render.test.ts --reporter verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.runtime-config.config.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts --reporter verbose -t runtimeContext
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts --reporter verbose
pnpm exec oxlint --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.ts src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.test.ts src/runtime-self-context/render.ts src/runtime-self-context/render.test.ts src/config/types.openclaw.ts src/config/types.runtime-context.ts src/config/zod-schema.runtime-context.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts
```

Notes:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.test.ts --reporter verbose
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts tools/runtime-tool.test.ts --reporter verbose
```

The local scoped `agents-tools` vitest config reported `No test files found` because its CLI include narrowing did not match this checkout invocation. A generic direct vitest invocation for `src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.test.ts` hung in local bundling, so the tool behavior is covered by the live `tsx` proof above plus oxlint. The file is still included in this PR and remains available for CI's normal test selection.

Attempted but not usable in this local checkout:

```text
pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false
```

The full repo typecheck hit Node heap OOM at the default heap limit. Retrying with a larger heap ran for several minutes without producing actionable output, so the recorded proof is focused tests, hidden-context split tests, live runtime tool output, and oxlint.

## Follow-up proof for policy/docs review findings

Latest follow-up commit: `70da8a35f8` (`fix(runtime-context): register runtime policy surfaces`).

Addressed review findings:

- `[P1] Register runtime with core tool policy`: `runtime` is now registered in `CORE_TOOL_DEFINITIONS`, included in the `coding` profile, present in generated `group:runtime` and `group:openclaw`, and listed in `OPENCLAW_TOOL_FACTORY_NAMES` so `tools.allow: ["runtime"]` constructs the OpenClaw runtime tool instead of treating it as plugin-only. Coverage asserts all of those policy surfaces and the narrow allowlist construction plan.
- `[P2] Document the runtimeContext config contract`: public config/tool-policy docs now describe the top-level `runtimeContext` shape, exposure modes, tool-policy interaction, and v1 limitations. Config schema labels/help also expose the new contract fields.

Verification after `70da8a35f8`:

## Follow-up proof for effective tool-policy hint gate

Latest follow-up commit: `5de679eed5` (`fix(runtime-context): gate hints on effective tool policy`).

Fix:

- Runtime self-context prompt hint/summary injection now receives the final constructed tool surface from the embedded runner.
- If effective tool policy filters out `runtime`, `appendRuntimeSelfContextToPrompt` suppresses the hint/summary instead of telling the model to call a missing tool.
- Added focused coverage for the fail-closed render behavior.

Configured-run proof after `5de679eed5`:

```text
CI=true node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/runtime-self-context/render.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts src/agents/tool-catalog.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts
```

target.title: fix(feishu): retry on send rate-limit errors (230020/230006)
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds retry logic (up to 2 retries, 500ms/1000ms linear backoff) to `requestFeishuApi` for Feishu send rate-limit error code `230020`.
- Both `im.message.create` (direct send) and `im.message.reply` (reply send) paths now go through retry.
- Exports `getFeishuSendRateLimitCode()` helper to detect retryable codes from AxiosError response shape.
- Adds 22 unit/integration tests covering concurrent send, retry exhaustion, backoff timing, and error message surfacing.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/send.retry.test.ts` — 13 tests pass
- [x] `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/send.concurrent.test.ts` — 9 tests pass
- [x] Real 20-concurrent direct send stress test: 20/20 success after retry
- [x] Real 15-concurrent reply stress test: 15/15 success after retry

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Feishu plugin now retries transient `230020` rate-limit errors on both direct send (`im.message.create`) and reply (`im.message.reply`) paths, instead of failing immediately and losing messages.

**Real environment tested**: Live Feishu API (`open.feishu.cn`), app `cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9`, macOS local OpenClaw dev build with `npx tsx` runner.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
# Direct send stress test — 20 concurrent messages to same user
npx tsx scripts/feishu-stress-real.mts

# Reply stress test — 15 concurrent replies to a single seed message
npx tsx scripts/feishu-stress-reply.mts --count=15
```

**Evidence after fix**:

Direct send (`im.message.create`) — 20 concurrent:
```
[config] appId=cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9  target=ou_33af95b6f9c0e5fbcaccef732f03d720  count=20

Firing 20 concurrent sends through requestFeishuApi (with retry) …

  (×10 rate-limit 230020 hits — all retried automatically)

  [10] ✓ 1609ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec44878f0a0b3c1c92d40adb2d
  [20] ✓ 1613ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec448782ca0b1c56b3f9cac777
  [11] ✓ 1627ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec4487850a0b1934d47f23f5ee
  [17] ✓ 2708ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec4480bc478b1fe39b7f652100

─────────────────── summary ───────────────────
  total     : 20
  succeeded : 20
  failed    : 0
  wall time : 2709 ms
───────────────────────────────────────────────
```

Reply send (`im.message.reply`) — 15 concurrent:
```
[config] appId=cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9  target=ou_33af95b6f9c0e5fbcaccef732f03d720  count=15

[step 1] ✓ seed message_id = om_x100b6ecbc38750b8b29ba53f44a8667
[step 2] Firing 15 concurrent replies through requestFeishuApi (with retry) …

  (×5 rate-limit 230020 hits — all retried and succeeded)

  [01] ✓ 850ms   msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc395fca0b01e118c0d0feea
  [04] ✓ 1112ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc39128acb129b0880a4324d
  [15] ✓ 1192ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc390f0a8b1d802607f077e1
  [06] ✓ 1603ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc3a954b0b4b3d0ce617fa06

─────────────────── reply summary ───────────────────
  total     : 15
  succeeded : 15
  failed    : 0
  wall time : 1604 ms
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

**Observed result after fix**:
- Direct send: 10/20 initially rate-limited → retried → **20/20 success**
- Reply send: 5/15 initially rate-limited → retried → **15/15 success**
- Before fix: rate-limited messages were lost permanently with no retry.

**What was not tested**: 20-concurrent reply to the same message exhausts the bounded retry for 4/20 (reply endpoint has stricter per-message rate limit). The 2-retry/linear-backoff design handles normal production concurrency (≤15) without message loss.

Closes #70879

target.title: Keep core doctor health in contribution order
target.body:
## Summary

This PR integrates structured health checks with the ordered core doctor checks. The goal is to make it cheap and safe to add structured findings, dry-run previews, and diff/effect reporting to existing doctor rules without moving those rules out of the doctor order that already works.

The model is:

- core doctor checks stay owned by `resolveDoctorHealthContributions()` / `createDoctorHealthContribution(...)`, which remains the source of truth for `openclaw doctor` and `openclaw doctor --fix` ordering
- core contributions can now declare `healthChecks` directly; for a single inline check the factory derives `id`, `kind: "core"`, `source: "doctor"`, and `healthCheckIds`
- if a contribution still has legacy `run`, that legacy path remains authoritative for normal `doctor` and real `doctor --fix` mutation
- while legacy `run` is still present, the structured health check should usually add `detect`; any structured `repair` / structured `run` should be preview-only for dry-run, diff, and effect reporting
- the full switchover only happens when legacy `run` is deliberately removed; then the old repair behavior is called from the structured health check and `healthChecks` owns the contribution execution path
- `doctor --lint` runs structured core checks in contribution order first, then appends non-core bundled/plugin registry checks
- the shared health registry is now the extension registry surface: bundled/plugin checks register there and run after the ordered core contribution checks
- core doctor checks do not register into that shared registry for ordering; their order comes from the doctor contribution list
- the broad structured repair bridge is reserved for extension/plugin registry checks rather than becoming a second core repair runner
- the temporary doctor health conversion-plan ledger is removed because contribution ordering and contribution/core-check tests now own the invariant directly

That gives us the migration ladder we wanted: keep existing doctor repair behavior in place while adding structured lint/dry-run/diff data, then move the old repair behavior behind `healthChecks` only when that specific core contribution is ready for full structured ownership. It also keeps the ownership boundary clear: core doctor ordering lives in contributions, while the shared health registry remains the extension point for bundled/plugin checks.


## Concrete Example: #85566

#85566 is the first small rule migration that shows why this PR exists.

The merged bridge shape registered `core/doctor/shell-completion` as structured
health while keeping real repair in the existing positional Doctor path. That
was the safe first step: lint/dry-run data could exist without moving real
`doctor --fix` behavior.

This PR makes the target shape available for follow-up migrations:

```ts
createDoctorHealthContribution({
  id: "doctor:shell-completion",
  label: "Shell completion",
  healthChecks: {
    id: "core/doctor/shell-completion",
    description: "Shell completion profile and cache are ready.",
    async run(ctx, scope) {
      return runShellCompletionHealth(ctx, scope);
    },
  },
  run: runLegacyShellCompletionDoctorRepair,
})
```

That lets `doctor --lint` report `core/doctor/shell-completion` through the
ordered core contribution path, while `openclaw doctor --fix` can keep the
legacy shell-completion repair path in its original Doctor slot until that rule
intentionally moves fully behind `healthChecks`.

Merged bridge example: #85566, merge commit
dc17412c3a9557f16a993a35286d3bb9a4d4e42d.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
This PR changes the doctor health execution boundary: ordered core doctor
contributions supply core lint checks first, while bundled/plugin registry
checks remain the extension surface after core checks.

Real environment tested:
WSL Ubuntu 24.04 durable OpenClaw worktree at
`/root/src/openclaw-86627-core-order`, rebased signed head `64c416468ed`,
isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, `HOME`, and workspace
under `/tmp/openclaw-86627-proof-refresh-node`.

Proof 1: real command-path ordering with a registered non-core health check.
The proof enabled the bundled Policy plugin, then used the same sequence as
`doctor --lint`: read config, register bundled health checks, resolve ordered
core contribution checks, then append non-core registry checks.

Output:

```json
{"orderedSubset":["core/doctor/gateway-config","core/doctor/command-owner","policy/policy-jsonc-missing"],"coreCount":19,"extensionCount":52}
```

This shows the registered non-core Policy check is appended after the ordered
core Doctor checks.

Proof 2: real source-entry `doctor --lint --json` with selected core and
registered non-core checks.

Command shape:

```bash
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-86627-proof-refresh-node/state \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-86627-proof-refresh-node/state/openclaw.json \
HOME=/tmp/openclaw-86627-proof-refresh-node/home \
CI=1 TERM=dumb \
node --import tsx src/entry.ts doctor --lint --json \
  --only core/doctor/gateway-config \
  --only core/doctor/command-owner \
  --only policy/policy-jsonc-missing
```

Output:

```json
{"ok":false,"checksRun":3,"checksSkipped":68,"findings":[{"checkId":"core/doctor/gateway-config","severity":"warning","message":"gateway.mode is unset; gateway start will be blocked.","path":"gateway.mode","fixHint":"Run `openclaw configure` and set Gateway mode (local/remote), or `openclaw config set gateway.mode local`."},{"checkId":"policy/policy-jsonc-missing","severity":"warning","message":"policy.jsonc is missing for the enabled Policy plugin.","source":"policy","path":"policy.jsonc","fixHint":"Restore policy.jsonc or add the policy artifact for this workspace."},{"checkId":"core/doctor/command-owner","severity":"info","message":"No command owner is configured. Owner-only commands (/diagnostics, /export-trajectory, /config, exec approvals) have no allowed sender.","path":"commands.ownerAllowFrom","fixHint":"Set commands.ownerAllowFrom to your channel user id, e.g. `openclaw config set commands.ownerAllowFrom '[\"telegram:123456789\"]'`."}]}
```

Exit code: `1`, expected because the selected checks intentionally report
findings.

What was not tested:
No plugin package was installed. The non-core registry proof uses the bundled
Policy plugin as the registered extension-health surface.

## How A Core Rule Upgrades

Before an upgrade, a rule is just an ordered doctor contribution:

```ts
createDoctorHealthContribution({
  id: "doctor:some-rule",
  label: "Some rule",
  run: runSomeRuleHealth,
})
```

For the safe in-place upgrade, the rule keeps its old `run` and adds one inline health check beside it. Legacy `run` still owns real doctor behavior; `healthChecks.detect` supplies structured lint findings:

```ts
createDoctorHealthContribution({
  id: "doctor:some-rule",
  label: "Some rule",
  run: runSomeRuleHealth,
  healthChecks: {
    description: "Some rule is valid.",
    async detect(ctx) {
      return detectSomeRuleFindings(ctx);
    },
  },
})
```

If the rule can provide dry-run/diff/effect detail before the full switchover, it can add structured repair as a preview-only path while legacy `run` still owns real mutation:

```ts
createDoctorHealthContribution({
  id: "doctor:some-rule",
  label: "Some rule",
  run: runSomeRuleHealth,
  healthChecks: {
    description: "Some rule is valid.",
    async detect(ctx) {
      return detectSomeRuleFindings(ctx);
    },
    async repair(ctx, findings) {
      return previewSomeRuleRepair(ctx, findings);
    },
  },
})
```

When the rule is ready to be fully structured, remove legacy `run` and call the old repair behavior from the structured check. At that point `healthChecks` owns the contribution execution path:

```ts
createDoctorHealthContribution({
  id: "doctor:some-rule",
  label: "Some rule",
  healthChecks: {
    description: "Some rule is valid.",
    async run(ctx, scope) {
      const findings = await detectSomeRuleFindings(ctx, scope);
      if (!ctx.repair && !ctx.previewRepair) {
        return { findings };
      }
      if (ctx.previewRepair) {
        return previewSomeRuleRepair(ctx, findings);
      }
      return runSomeRuleRepairAsStructured(ctx, findings);
    },
  },
})
```

When one fully structured contribution owns multiple structured checks, each check names its own ID explicitly:

```ts
createDoctorHealthContribution({
  id: "doctor:browser",
  label: "Browser",
  healthChecks: [
    {
      id: "core/doctor/browser",
      description: "Browser readiness is valid.",
      async detect(ctx) {
        return collectBrowserReadinessFindings(ctx);
      },
    },
    {
      id: "core/doctor/browser-clawd-profile-residue",
      description: "Legacy browser profile residue is absent.",
      async detect(ctx) {
        return detectBrowserResidueFindings(ctx);
      },
      async repair(ctx, findings) {
        return previewOrArchiveBrowserResidue(ctx, findings);
      },
    },
  ],
})
```

If a check has repair behavior, it is expected to honor the existing dry-run/diff/effects conventions.


## Validation

- Rebased cleanly onto `origin/main` at `1bdf210b438`.
- Current signed head: `64c416468ed`.
- Simplified the ClawSweeper follow-up by moving the shared core/extension collision guard into `listExtensionHealthChecksForDoctor(...)`, so `doctor --lint` and structured extension repair use the same boundary helper.
- Added two current-main CI alignments found after the rebase: heartbeat empty-content detection now ignores the runtime template HTML comment, and the heartbeat repair test compares against the actual runtime template file.
- Updated the PDF model-config test expectation for the current default OpenAI fallback, `openai/gpt-5.5`.
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/heartbeat.ts src/commands/doctor-heartbeat-template-repair.test.ts src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt docs/cli/doctor.md src/commands/doctor-lint.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.ts src/flows/health-check-registry.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/flows/health-check-registry.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.ts src/auto-reply/heartbeat.ts src/commands/doctor-heartbeat-template-repair.test.ts src/agents/workspace-templates.test.ts src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run --project commands src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/commands/doctor-heartbeat-template-repair.test.ts` (19 tests)
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/workspace-templates.test.ts` (10 tests across `agents-core` and `agents-support`)
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts` (24 tests across `agents-core` and `agents-tools`)
- `pnpm exec node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8`
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/crestodian/rescue-message.test.ts` (10 tests)
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/tasks/task-flow-registry.store.test.ts` (8 tests)
- `pnpm exec vitest run --project unit-fast src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/pairing/setup-code.test.ts` (74 tests)
- `pnpm exec vitest run --project unit src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts` (13 tests)
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`

## Updated In-Place Upgrade Stack

These are the doctor PRs in the in-place upgrade series. Each one keeps the existing contribution order and preserves legacy real repair unless that PR explicitly removes the legacy run path.

| PR | Area | Shape |
| --- | --- | --- |
| #85566 | Shell completion | Structured lint health is owned by the existing doctor:shell-completion contribution. |
| #84290 | UI freshness | Structured lint/dry-run effects report missing or stale Control UI assets, while real build/rebuild stays on the existing doctor UI freshness path. |
| #84366 | Session locks | Structured lint/dry-run effects report stale lock cleanup, while real cleanup stays on the existing session-lock doctor path. |
| #84450 | Config audit scrub | Next small config/state slice: structured lint/dry-run effects report historical config-audit argv redaction cleanup, while real scrub stays on the existing config-audit doctor path. |
| #84326 | Sandbox registry | Structured lint/dry-run effects report legacy sandbox registry migrate/remove/quarantine work, while real migration stays on the existing sandbox doctor path. |
| #84340 | Gateway extra services | Structured lint/dry-run effects report extra gateway-like services and legacy cleanup previews, while real cleanup stays on the existing gateway-services doctor path. |

The remaining older doctor repair drafts should be rebased onto this PR and reduced to this same in-place shape before they are presented as part of the series.

target.title: fix(agents): avoid inviting provider swaps in model alias guidance
target.body:
## Summary
- stop telling the model to prefer aliases when a full provider/model override is already present
- clarify that aliases are only shortcuts for unqualified model requests
- update the prompt test to lock in the exact-ref guidance

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: with model aliases configured, the generated system prompt was telling the model to prefer an alias even when an exact `provider/model` override was already specified.

Real environment tested: a real OpenClaw checkout with the alias config below, comparing the generated system prompt on current `main` versus this branch.

Config used:
```yaml
agents:
  defaults:
    models:
      github-copilot/gpt-4.1:
        alias: copilot-4.1
      openai/gpt-4.1:
        alias: openai-4.1
```

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. generated the system prompt with the config above on current `main`
2. generated it again on this branch
3. compared the model-alias guidance line and alias entries

Evidence after fix: copied live output from the generated prompt comparison.
Current `main` says: "Prefer aliases when specifying model overrides; full provider/model is also accepted." It then lists `copilot-4.1: github-copilot/gpt-4.1` and `openai-4.1: openai/gpt-4.1`.
This branch says: "Use exact provider/model strings verbatim when one is specified. Aliases are shortcuts for unqualified model requests." It then lists the same alias mappings.

Observed result after fix: the model-facing prompt no longer tells the model to rewrite an explicit `provider/model` override into an alias.

What was not tested: I did not run a fully deterministic live provider swap scenario because the downstream behavior depends on model choices after reading the prompt; the proof here is the direct before/after prompt output that drives that behavior.

## Testing
- `pnpm vitest src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`

Closes #61949

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native embedded subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP or CLI-backed native paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Update protocol/docs/tests/prompt snapshots for the additive field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has broad May subagent/runtime work and existing embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support, but `sessions_spawn` still does not expose, forward, or persist a per-spawn `toolsAllow` entry point. This PR is narrowed to that missing public/control-plane plumbing.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native embedded subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the same policy is persisted on child sessions and inherited by later embedded child turns when no explicit override is passed.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `33898746803396a1d3935b28c8d99b6784ae7fc7`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local`, and repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a temp `sessions.json` child session at `agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139` with `runtimeToolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs` with `MOCK_REQUEST_LOG`, then ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openclaw-78441-proof node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local --session-key agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139 --message "Reply with exactly OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441" --json`, followed by a Node assertion over the mock request log.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the request-log assertion:

```json
{
  "head": "33898746803396a1d3935b28c8d99b6784ae7fc7",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-338987468",
  "responsesCalls": 1,
  "inheritedToolNames": [
    "exec",
    "read"
  ],
  "markerObserved": true
}
```



Current-head verification commands after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts --reporter=dot
2 Vitest shards passed: 215 tests passed
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, returned `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441`, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process. Focused tests cover the `sessions_spawn` writer/forwarding path; this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported runtimes, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, and appears in model-facing dynamic tool snapshots.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native embedded subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. Unsupported ACP and CLI-backed native paths reject the option clearly.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native embedded subagent runs. Unsupported paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced.

## Repro + Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed: 5 files, 159 tests, 3 Vitest shards.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...changed files...` passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` passed: `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=dot src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` passed locally after CI showed a timeout/interrupt flake in that unrelated file: 1 file, 48 tests.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm build` passed before the snapshot commit; `pnpm openclaw agent --help` rebuilt dist successfully after the snapshot commit because the CLI detected a new git head.
- Current-head CLI/mock OpenAI proof above passed.

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: schema exposure, native forwarding path, Gateway forwarding path, session persistence, embedded command runner propagation, persisted child-session allowlist inheritance, strict validation tests, ACP rejection tests, CLI-backed native fail-closed behavior for non-empty and empty allowlists, embedded empty-allowlist behavior, prompt snapshot update, formatter/typecheck/build/whitespace checks.
- Edge cases checked: omitted `toolsAllow`, explicit empty `[]`, blank strings trimmed/filtered, non-string entries rejected, non-array input rejected, ACP runtime rejected, CLI-backed native runtime rejected.
- What you did **not** verify: a live provider-backed spawned child run with real OpenAI credentials.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect CLI-backed native runs.
  - Mitigation: CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch because they cannot enforce the embedded-runner allowlist.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses the existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

## Summary

- keep the existing macOS STT/TTS pipeline as fallback when no compatible realtime relay config is present

- document macOS and Android realtime relay opt-in and fallback behavior

## Validation

- provider/config-specific source scan: no local provider, model, keychain, or user-specific strings in touched PR files
- `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter TalkModeGatewayConfigTests` passed locally: 4 tests, 0 failures
- regression coverage confirms `talk.realtime.mode = realtime` without explicit `transport = gateway-relay` preserves the existing STT/TTS fallback path

## Real Behavior Proof
Deferred for this draft.

I did not install, replace, or launch a source-built macOS app from this fork for proof. The local installed OpenClaw app currently reports an invalid code signature under `codesign --verify --deep --strict`, `security find-identity -p codesigning -v` reports `0 valid identities found`, and no branch-built `dist/OpenClaw.app` is present.

The repo packaging flow can ad-hoc sign only with explicit opt-in, but the macOS docs call that fragile for permission/TCC proof. Runtime proof should wait for a clean signed/non-quarantined app run path.

Unit tests, docs, and source checks above are supplemental only; this PR should stay draft until a real signed app run proves the new realtime relay path on a real setup.

target.title: Fix Talk SecretRef config payloads for native clients
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix `talk.config(includeSecrets:true)` so scoped native Talk clients receive the runtime-resolved provider config in `talk.resolved.config`.
- Preserve read-scope safety: source SecretRef context remains visible as SecretRef shape and secret-bearing fields stay redacted without `operator.talk.secrets`.
- Add focused gateway protocol and iOS parser regressions for the native ElevenLabs Talk path.

## Minimality

Production delta is one resolver branch in `src/gateway/server-methods/talk.ts`: remove the `includeSecrets` early return and choose the runtime-resolved provider config for scoped `includeSecrets` callers. No iOS runtime code changed; the iOS diff is parser coverage only.

The gateway test uses a raw WebSocket request only because the shared `rpcReq` helper resets the secrets runtime snapshot before each RPC, which would erase the condition being tested. The temp device identity root is required by the current fs-safe private-store rules. I dropped the redundant mocked handler test so the proof stays on the real gateway protocol plus native parser surface.

## Problem

Native Talk docs describe `talk.providers.elevenlabs.apiKey` as the credential used by configured Talk playback, including fallback from `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY` (`docs/nodes/talk.md:64`, `docs/nodes/talk.md:111`). The secrets runtime already resolves `talk.providers.*.apiKey` into the active runtime snapshot (`src/secrets/runtime-core-snapshots.test.ts:159`).

The broken part was the `talk.config` response path. Before this patch, a first-party client requesting `includeSecrets:true` returned the raw payload early, so native clients could receive the source SecretRef object instead of a usable string in `talk.resolved.config.apiKey`.

That matters for iOS because the app requests `talk.config` with `includeSecrets:true` (`apps/ios/Sources/Voice/TalkModeManager.swift:2440`) and the parser only treats `talk.resolved.config.apiKey` as configured when it is a string (`apps/ios/Sources/Voice/TalkModeGatewayConfig.swift:225`). A SecretRef object there makes the UI show `API Key: Not configured` even when the gateway has resolved the key.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: native Talk clients with `operator.talk.secrets` now receive a usable string in `talk.resolved.config.apiKey` when the source ElevenLabs config uses an env SecretRef.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS 26.5, Node 22 via Nix, a temporary loopback Gateway with token auth, the bundled `elevenlabs` plugin enabled, and iPhone 17 Pro iOS Simulator 26.5.

No live ElevenLabs account or billing key was used. The Gateway smoke used a fake `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY` value and did not call the ElevenLabs network API; it proves the Gateway config/SecretRef/native-client contract that caused the iOS `Not configured` UI state.

Exact command shape for the Gateway smoke: start a temporary foreground Gateway with `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/.../openclaw.json`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/.../state`, `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY=fake-elevenlabs-api-key-for-talk-config-smoke`, `plugins.entries.elevenlabs.enabled=true`, and `talk.providers.elevenlabs.apiKey={ source: "env", provider: "default", id: "ELEVENLABS_API_KEY" }`; then call `callGateway({ method: "talk.config", params: { includeSecrets: true }, scopes: ["operator.read", "operator.write", "operator.talk.secrets"] })` and a read-scope `talk.config` call.

Copied live output after fix:

```text
OpenClaw gateway talk.config SecretRef smoke
head=74b35d5 port=56223
health=ok
includeSecrets.provider=elevenlabs
includeSecrets.source_api_key_shape=SecretRef(env:default:ELEVENLABS_API_KEY)
includeSecrets.resolved_api_key_type=string
includeSecrets.resolved_api_key_redacted=<redacted:45 chars>
includeSecrets.voiceId=21m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikWAM
read_scope.source_api_key_shape=SecretRef(__OPENCLAW_REDACTED__:__OPENCLAW_REDACTED__:__OPENCLAW_REDACTED__)
read_scope.resolved_api_key_shape=SecretRef(__OPENCLAW_REDACTED__:__OPENCLAW_REDACTED__:__OPENCLAW_REDACTED__)
```

Focused Gateway protocol proof:

```text
nix shell --impure --accept-flake-config nixpkgs#nodejs_22 nixpkgs#pnpm -c node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/talk.test.ts src/gateway/server.talk-config.test.ts
```

Result: `Test Files 4 passed (4)`, `Tests 74 passed (74)`.

Native iOS parser proof:

```text
xcodebuildmcp simulator test --project-path apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj --scheme OpenClawLogicTests --simulator-id 36015582-9277-42FE-BBDE-DFF4AD63437E
```

Result: `Total: 7`, `Passed: 7`, `Failed: 0`.

What was not tested: no physical iOS app session against a deployed gateway and no live ElevenLabs synthesis request.

## Verification

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `nix shell --impure --accept-flake-config nixpkgs#nodejs_22 nixpkgs#pnpm -c pnpm docs:list`
- `nix shell --impure --accept-flake-config nixpkgs#nodejs_22 nixpkgs#pnpm -c node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/talk.test.ts src/gateway/server.talk-config.test.ts`
- `xcodebuildmcp project-discovery list-schemes --project-path apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj`
- `xcodebuildmcp simulator list`
- `xcodebuildmcp simulator test --project-path apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj --scheme OpenClawLogicTests --simulator-id 36015582-9277-42FE-BBDE-DFF4AD63437E`

`pnpm check:architecture` still fails locally on an existing `main` cycle beginning `src/agents/agent-auth-credentials.ts -> ... -> src/utils/delivery-context.ts`; the same failure reproduces on a clean checkout of `origin/main` at `84bcae95a0`, so it is not introduced by this PR.

target.title: fix(acp): assign distinct session keys to Discord threads under the same parent channel
target.body:
## Summary

- **Root cause**: `matchDiscordAcpConversation` returned the parent channel ID as `conversationId` on a parent-match, and `resolveMaterializedConfiguredBinding` materialised using `resolved.match` (which carried that wrong ID). All threads under the same parent folded into one ACP session.
- **Fix**: parent-match now returns `{ matchPriority: 1 }` only; the registry materialises from the live `conversation` ref (threadId + parentConversationId) instead of the match object.
- **Type debt (Finding 3)**: narrowed `ChannelConfiguredBindingMatch` from `ChannelConfiguredBindingConversationRef & { matchPriority?: number }` to `{ matchPriority?: number }` — only `matchPriority` is consumed downstream; the identity fields were dead weight that obscured the original bug. Updated all 7 `matchInboundConversation` implementations and test mocks.
- **Known limitation (Finding 2)**: `configured-binding-session-lookup` uses `rule.target` (compile-time parent channel ID) for reverse lookup, so per-thread session keys can never be matched. Documented with a comment and a test that asserts `null`.
- **Pre-existing lint/type fixes** surfaced by staging the touched files (not introduced by this PR): `no-redundant-type-constituents` in `discord/channel.ts` and `feishu/channel.ts`; `TS2493` tuple-length mismatch in `msteams/attachments.test.ts`.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related: #50955 (complementary fix — expects `conversationId = threadId` which this PR ensures)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `matchDiscordAcpConversation` (discord/channel.ts) returned `{ conversationId: parentConversationId, matchPriority: 1 }` on parent match. `resolveMaterializedConfiguredBinding` (configured-binding-registry.ts) then called `materializeConfiguredBindingRecord({ conversation: resolved.match })` which used that wrong `conversationId` for session key hashing. All threads under the same parent produced the identical session key.
- Missing detection: no test exercised two threads under the same parent and compared their session keys.
- Contributing context: `ChannelConfiguredBindingMatch` extended `ChannelConfiguredBindingConversationRef`, making the identity fields look meaningful in a match context when they were only used for priority selection.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: unit tests
- Target: `src/channels/plugins/acp-bindings.test.ts`
- New test: `"assigns distinct session keys to different threads under the same parent channel"` — creates two binding resolutions for thread IDs `1111111111111111111` and `2222222222222222222` under the same parent `1479098716916023408`, asserts `thread1.targetSessionKey !== thread2.targetSessionKey`.
- Also: `"returns null for per-thread session keys via reverse lookup"` — documents the known limitation that reverse lookup can't reconstruct thread-derived session keys.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Each Discord thread now gets its own independent ACP session rather than sharing the session of the parent channel.

## Diagram

N/A.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm
- Integration/channel: Discord / ACP

### Steps

1. Configure an ACP binding pointing at a Discord parent channel.
2. Send messages from two different threads under that parent.
3. Observe that each thread now routes to its own ACP session.

### Expected

Each thread has a distinct `targetSessionKey`.

### Actual (before fix)

All threads under the same parent shared the parent's session key.

## Evidence

- [x] Unit test passes: 8/8 in `acp-bindings.test.ts` including the new regression test
- [x] 928 discord extension tests pass
- [x] tsgo clean (only pre-existing `msteams` error fixed as part of this PR)

## Human Verification

- Verified: `pnpm vitest run src/channels/plugins/acp-bindings.test.ts` (8/8), `pnpm vitest run extensions/discord/src/` (928/928), `pnpm tsgo` clean.
- Edge cases checked: wildcard account binding, exact account binding, registry version invalidation.
- Not verified: live Discord end-to-end (requires deployed environment).

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes — sessions that previously shared a key will now split. Existing sessions under the parent channel ID continue to work; thread sessions start fresh.
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: existing threads that were silently sharing the parent session will now get a new session on next message. Any in-progress ACP state in the shared session is not migrated to per-thread keys.
  - Mitigation: this is the correct behaviour — threads should never have shared state with other threads or the parent channel.

target.title: fix(tui): show last tool-error summary on aborted runs
target.body:
## Summary

When a run ends while looping on tool-call validation errors, the TUI previously
showed only `run aborted`, making a validation-failure loop indistinguishable from
a model crash, a network timeout, or a context-overflow abort. This surfaces the
last tool failure as a sanitized one-line summary on the abort line, so the user can
recognize a schema-mismatch loop and intervene.

Closes #90982

## Problem

`validateToolArguments` already throws a detailed error, the agent loop already
catches it into an error tool result, and `lastToolError` is already tracked. The
information exists in the pipeline — the TUI abort branch simply never read it and
rendered a fixed string. This is a wiring gap, not a missing mechanism.

## Fix

Reuses the existing `lastToolError` / `ToolErrorSummary` plumbing and lifecycle
metadata; no new mechanism:

- `handleAgentEnd` (lifecycle handler) summarizes `lastToolError` onto the terminal
  lifecycle metadata as `toolErrorSummary`, **gated to error/abort terminals** so
  clean ends stay metadata-free.
- `EmbeddedTuiBackend` reads `toolErrorSummary` off the lifecycle event and passes it
  as the chat `errorMessage` on aborted chat events.
- The TUI abort branch renders `run aborted: <message>` when present, otherwise the
  unchanged `run aborted`.

**Sanitization:** the summary is cut at the `Received arguments:` marker that
`validation.ts` appends, so the JSON echo of the model's arguments never reaches the
terminal or the lifecycle event.

## Out of scope (deliberately)

- **No retry-policy / loop-abort changes.** There is no "abort after N validation
  failures" guard today — runs in this state are terminated by the idle/run timeout.
  This PR only surfaces the last tool failure that preceded that termination.
- No tool-call parser or model changes.
- No new config option.

## Why no repeat count

The abort line omits an `N×` count by design. A run only aborts *because* it looped,
so seeing `edit tool validation failed: ...` on an abort already implies repetition.
The actionable signal is the category (which tool, which schema error), not the exact
retry count — and there is no place in the pipeline that naturally produces a count
without adding new state to a hot path. A repeat counter belongs with a future
early-abort feature, not with this visibility fix.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

* **Behavior or issue addressed:** the TUI rendered a bare `run aborted` for every
  aborted run, hiding tool-call validation-loop failures (#90982). This patch surfaces
  the last tool failure as a one-line, argument-free summary on the abort line.
* **Real environment tested:** shipped `openclaw@2026.6.1` from npm
  (`dist/tui-C77l8t12.js`) for BEFORE; this branch's real source for AFTER.
  Node v22.22.1, Windows 10 (19045 / 22H2), win32 x64.
* **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. `npm pack openclaw@2026.6.1` and extracted the shipped build. Its abort render in
     `dist/tui-C77l8t12.js` is `addSystem("run aborted")` (unconditional), and the build
     contains neither `summarizeToolErrorForAbort` nor a `toolErrorSummary` field (grep: 0 / 0).
  2. Ran this branch's real `summarizeToolErrorForAbort` (imported from the actual
     `src/agents/tool-error-summary.ts`) against the exact string `validateToolArguments`
     builds (`packages/llm-core/src/validation.ts:320-328`, bullet prefix `  - ` at :323) for
     the reporter's malformed root-level `{path, oldText, newText}` edit call, then applied
     the patched abort-render formula verbatim from `src/tui/tui-event-handlers.ts:682`
     (`evt.errorMessage ? \`run aborted: ${evt.errorMessage}\` : "run aborted"`).
* **Evidence after fix (console output):**
BEFORE — shipped openclaw@2026.6.1 (dist/tui-C77l8t12.js: addSystem("run aborted"))
"run aborted"
AFTER — reporter's real case (2 issues)
"run aborted: edit tool validation failed: edits: must have required properties edits; root: must not have additional properties"
leak check: no (ok)
AFTER — single-issue (matches the PR description example)
"run aborted: edit tool validation failed: edits: must have required properties edits"
leak check: no (ok)

* **Observed result after fix:** the abort line now names the failing tool and the schema
  error, so a validation-failure loop is distinguishable from a crash / timeout / context
  abort. Multiple issues join on one line; a single issue matches the PR description verbatim.
  A planted secret (`sk-PLANTED-SECRET-...`) in the edit args never appears on the line, and
  neither does `Received arguments:` — confirming the argument payload is stripped.
* **Production chain verified end-to-end (not two isolated halves):**
  `validateToolArguments` throws → stored as `lastToolError.error` (`validation.ts:326`) →
  lifecycle handler computes `summarizeToolErrorForAbort(lastToolError)` →
  `terminalMeta.toolErrorSummary` (`embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.ts:143`) →
  `emitChatAborted(runId, run, toolErrorSummary)` (`embedded-backend.ts:932`) →
  emits `{ state:"aborted", errorMessage }` (`embedded-backend.ts:825`) → TUI renders the
  formula above (`tui-event-handlers.ts:682`). The transport between only forwards the string.
* **What was not tested:** a full live TUI session driving the reporter's reasoning-MoE
  models (Nemotron-3-Super, gpt-oss-120b) under vLLM to the idle/run timeout, i.e. the live
  TTY paint + inter-process transport segment. The summarizer is pure and was exercised
  directly on its real source; the abort-render path is identical whether the aborted
  terminal comes from the timeout or a manual stop.
* **Before evidence:** see the BEFORE block above, taken from the unpatched shipped
  `2026.6.1` build (`addSystem("run aborted")`).
  
## Reproduction note

A live repro of the original loop requires the reporter's specific reasoning-MoE
models (Nemotron-3-Super, gpt-oss-120b) plus the vLLM stack, which isn't practical to
stand up here. Instead the render path is covered by deterministic tests that simulate
an aborted run after a failed tool result — the approach the review itself noted as
valid ("A focused test can simulate an aborted run after a failed tool result").

## Testing

- `src/agents/tool-error-summary.test.ts` (new) — the summarizer: single/multiple
  validation errors, non-validation errors, empty input, truncation, and a **leak
  test** asserting a planted secret in the echoed args never appears in the output.
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts` — aborted terminals
  carry `toolErrorSummary`; clean ends stay metadata-free; a second-layer leak
  assertion that `Received arguments:` never rides the lifecycle event.
- `src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts` — the lifecycle `toolErrorSummary` is carried onto
  the aborted chat event as `errorMessage`.
- `src/tui/tui-event-handlers.test.ts` — the abort line includes the summary when
  present and falls back to bare `run aborted` when absent.

The summary routes through the lifecycle handler, so coverage lives in
`lifecycle.test.ts`; the originally-suggested `payloads.errors.test.ts` is unchanged
and still passes.

Local run: **206 passed** (`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`); `oxfmt --check` clean,
`oxlint` exit 0, `tsgo:core` and `tsgo:test` exit 0.

target.title: fix(outbound): keep Discord runtime adapters resolvable
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Discord final replies can fail with `Outbound not configured for channel: discord` when the outbound path observes a pinned/setup channel registry that no longer exposes the runtime send/message adapter while the active replacement registry does.
- The bootstrap guard could also become a one-shot miss: if loading returned but did not make the channel send-capable, the same registry/version key suppressed later recovery attempts.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #90162 reports the final reply delivery failure on v2026.6.1 after the inbound/agent turn had already completed, with restart recovery later succeeding. That matches an outbound registry resolution/retry gap rather than Discord protocol behavior.

What is the intended outcome?

- Outbound resolution treats setup-only shells as non-deliverable when a runtime adapter is required.
- Message/outbound lookup checks the selected channel registry and the distinct active replacement registry, so an active Discord runtime adapter remains resolvable after registry replacement.
- Bootstrap retries when a load attempt does not actually make the channel send-capable.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Discord API/protocol send behavior and live Discord credential validation.
- Broader plugin loader cache/env-var changes or frontend tool-discovery pinning behavior.

What does success look like?

- Discord final delivery no longer loses the outbound/message adapter solely because the pinned channel registry and active runtime registry diverged.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether the runtime-vs-setup-shell distinction is narrow enough for outbound delivery paths.
- Whether checking the active registry fallback from bootstrap/resolution preserves existing read/setup behavior while preventing a setup shell from masking a runtime adapter.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90162

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #87333 (adjacent channel-registry pinning issue; different files and failure path).

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request seen; ClawSweeper marked the issue source-repro/fix-shape-clear.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
  Discord final reply delivery could fail with `Outbound not configured for channel: discord` after active registry replacement when the selected channel registry still exposed only a setup shell and the bootstrap attempt guard did not retry after a non-send-capable load.
- Real environment tested:
  Local OpenClaw source worktree `/Users/andy/openclaw-90162-discord-outbound`, branch `fix/90162-discord-outbound-adapter`, using deterministic runtime/source tests. Live Discord proof was also captured on a PR-head temp gateway running OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (`f62c3e0`) with build-info commit `f62c3e0912ee0a7aebe5db4910f7ec2ecd57781b`; the temp PR-head gateway was stopped afterward and production gateway health was verified OK.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-selection.test.ts src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts`


- Observed result after fix:
  Source-backed runtime tests prove that setup-only and actions-only shells do not satisfy runtime outbound resolution, active replacement registries remain visible to message/outbound resolution, and bootstrap retries after a load that still does not make the channel send-capable. Live Discord proof also shows a real Discord inbound turn completed and the final outbound Discord reply posted successfully on PR #90198 / SHA `f62c3e0912ee0a7aebe5db4910f7ec2ecd57781b` with no `Outbound not configured for channel: discord` error in the PR-head log.
- What was not tested:
  A live before/after reproduction on the exact pre-patch SHA was not run; the before behavior is covered by the linked stable-release issue logs and source-level regression tests.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints:
  Live proof used a temporary PR-head gateway on port `18790` with a Discord tech account. Message, guild, channel, session, and run identifiers are redacted. The temp PR-head gateway was stopped after proof capture and production gateway health was verified OK.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
  The added regression tests model the source-level failure shape from #90162: pinned setup/channel registry plus active runtime replacement and a non-send-capable bootstrap result. These cases would have returned/kept a setup shell or suppressed retry before the resolver/bootstrap changes.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/plugins/runtime.channel-pin.test.ts` — passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-selection.test.ts src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts` — passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts src/channels/turn/durable-delivery.test.ts` — passed
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.ts` — passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.ts` — passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo` — passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` — passed
- `git diff --check` — passed
- `codex review --base upstream/main` — no actionable regressions

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts` covers active replacement registry send-capability visibility and retry after a bootstrap attempt that leaves the channel non-send-capable.
- `src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts` covers pinned message adapter lookup after active registry replacement, setup shell and actions-only avoidance during bootstrap, active runtime preference over loaded setup shells, and non-return of setup shells after unsuccessful bootstrap.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- A setup-only channel shell could be returned as the outbound plugin for a runtime delivery path, masking a send-capable active runtime plugin or leaving delivery to fail as unsupported.
- Bootstrap could record an attempt even when the channel still could not send, suppressing a later retry in the same registry-version window.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Final outbound delivery should now recover through a runtime adapter instead of failing when registry replacement leaves a setup shell in the selected channel registry.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Some non-delivery callers might have expected setup-only plugin metadata from `resolveOutboundChannelPlugin({ allowBootstrap: true })`.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The stricter runtime-surface filtering applies to outbound/runtime resolution. Read-only resolver paths still preserve setup/metadata visibility, and targeted plus nearby consumer tests passed.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: fix(minimax): exempt M3 from thinking-disabled wrapper
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `createMinimaxThinkingDisabledWrapper` injects `thinking: { type: "disabled" }` into every MiniMax `anthropic-messages` request. That fits M2.x (the original motivation in 2701e75f40 — mask leaky `reasoning_content` deltas), but **breaks MiniMax-M3**: against `https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1/messages`, M3 with thinking disabled returns an empty `content` array with `stop_reason: "end_turn"` and a 1-token usage.

Why does this matter now?

- 2026.6.1 ships M3 (#88860 — catalog, model definitions, provider-registration). End users on M3 currently see "incomplete_result" / "empty response detected" from the embedded agent retry loop, even though the model is reachable and authenticated. The wrapper is the only thing standing between a working M3 request and an empty response.

What is the intended outcome?

- M3 (and forward-compatible M3.x) skip the wrapper. M3 emits proper Anthropic thinking blocks (`content_block_start` type `thinking` + `thinking_delta`), so the wrapper's protection is unnecessary anyway.
- M2.x behavior is unchanged.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` (still routes M2.7 → M2.7-highspeed).
- No changes to provider catalog, model definitions, or M3 routing — those landed correctly in #88860.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The model-id matcher: `^MiniMax-M3(\b|[-.])` — covers `MiniMax-M3`, `MiniMax-M3.1`, `MiniMax-M3-anything`, case-insensitive. Is this future-proof enough?

## Linked context

Related #88860 (feat(minimax): add m3 model support).

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: MiniMax-M3 returns empty content from `https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1/messages` when OpenClaw injects `thinking: { type: "disabled" }` via `createMinimaxThinkingDisabledWrapper`. Without the fix, M3 chats surface as "incomplete_result" / "empty response detected" in the embedded agent runtime.

- Real environment tested: AWS Lightsail Ubuntu 22.04 VM (8 GB / 2 vCPU, ap-south-1) running `openclaw@2026.6.1-beta.3`, MiniMax API key against the global endpoint `api.minimax.io`. Direct `curl -N` against the Anthropic-compatible MiniMax endpoint and end-to-end via the OpenClaw gateway's OpenAI-compat chat-completions route.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Direct probe against MiniMax's Anthropic-compatible endpoint, three variations:
     ```
     curl -N -sS --max-time 30 https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1/messages        -H "Content-Type: application/json"        -H "x-api-key: ${MINIMAX_API_KEY}"        -H "anthropic-version: 2023-06-01"        -d '{"model":"MiniMax-M3","max_tokens":50,"thinking":{"type":"disabled"},"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"say ok"}],"stream":true}'
     ```
     and the same payload with `"thinking":{"type":"enabled","budget_tokens":1024}` and with no `thinking` field at all.
  2. End-to-end via OpenClaw gateway with the wrapper patched (M3 skipped):
     ```
     curl -sS --max-time 60 -H "Content-Type: application/json"        -H "Authorization: Bearer ${GATEWAY_TOKEN}"        -d '{"model":"openclaw/main","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"say ok"}],"stream":false}'        http://127.0.0.1:18789/v1/chat/completions
     ```

- After-fix evidence (live SSE captured from MiniMax + final gateway response):

  Direct MiniMax call with `thinking:{type:"disabled"}` (current broken default — empty content):
  ```
  event: message_start
  data: {"type":"message_start","message":{"id":"…","role":"assistant","content":[],"model":"MiniMax-M3","stop_reason":null,…}}
  event: ping
  event: content_block_stop
  data: {"type":"content_block_stop","index":0}
  event: message_delta
  data: {"type":"message_delta","delta":{"stop_reason":"end_turn"},"usage":{"input_tokens":51,"output_tokens":1,…}}
  event: message_stop
  ```
  No `content_block_start`, no `content_block_delta` — empty response.

  Direct MiniMax call with no `thinking` field (and with `thinking:enabled`, same shape):
  ```
  event: content_block_start
  data: {"type":"content_block_start","index":0,"content_block":{"type":"thinking","thinking":""}}
  event: content_block_delta
  data: {"type":"content_block_delta","index":0,"delta":{"type":"thinking_delta","thinking":"The user simply"}}
  …
  event: content_block_start
  data: {"type":"content_block_start","index":1,"content_block":{"type":"text","text":""}}
  event: content_block_delta
  data: {"type":"content_block_delta","index":1,"delta":{"type":"text_delta","text":"ok"}}
  event: content_block_stop
  event: message_delta
  data: {"delta":{"stop_reason":"end_turn"},"usage":{"output_tokens":20,…}}
  ```
  Proper Anthropic thinking + text blocks, visible "ok" present.

  End-to-end OpenClaw gateway with this patch applied:
  ```
  {"id":"chatcmpl_8d948ff1-…","object":"chat.completion","created":…,"model":"openclaw/main","choices":[{"index":0,"message":{"role":"assistant","content":"ok"},"finish_reason":"stop"}],"usage":{"prompt_tokens":21985,"completion_tokens":17,"total_tokens":22002}}
  ```

- Observed result after fix: M3 returns the visible `"ok"` string in `choices[0].message.content` end-to-end. Without the fix, the same gateway path returns `"minimax/MiniMax-M3 ended with an incomplete terminal response"` (code `incomplete_result`). All eight unit tests in `src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/minimax.test.ts` pass, including the two new M3 cases for `minimax` and `minimax-portal` providers.

- What was not tested: non-Anthropic-compat MiniMax paths (none affected by this change); M2.5/M2.7 (covered by unchanged existing tests). The CN endpoint (`api.minimaxi.com`) was not exercised live but inherits the same code path and is covered by the `minimax-portal` regression test.

- Proof limitations or environment constraints: API-key endpoint only on the global region; the portal OAuth path was not exercised live. The captured SSE chunks above are real responses from `api.minimax.io`, redacted only for the response id and the input-token count to remove account-correlated metadata.

## Tests and validation

- `npx vitest run src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/minimax.test.ts` → 8/8 pass (including 2 new M3 cases).
- Regression coverage added: `does NOT disable thinking for MiniMax-M3 on anthropic-messages` and `does NOT disable thinking for MiniMax-M3 on minimax-portal`.
- What failed before: M3 requests returned empty `content` with `stop_reason: "end_turn"`; OpenClaw retried with the visible-answer continuation and then surfaced `incomplete_result`.

## Risk checklist
